title: Ragas documentation
note: Official docs describe Ragas as a library for systematic evaluation loops, LLM-app metrics, test set generation, and integrations with frameworks such as LangChain and LlamaIndex.

title: DeepEval 5-min Quickstart
note: Official quickstart shows local evaluation through test cases, metrics, and the deepeval test run workflow, with optional Confident AI reporting.

title: Phoenix: Evaluate RAG
note: Official Phoenix cookbook demonstrates building a RAG pipeline, tracing it, and evaluating retrieval and response quality with Phoenix Evals.

title: LangSmith: Evaluate a RAG application
note: Official LangSmith tutorial covers datasets, running a RAG app over examples, and metrics such as correctness, relevance, groundedness, and retrieval relevance.

title: Promptfoo: Evaluating RAG pipelines
note: Official guide frames RAG evaluation as separate retrieval and generation checks, with output-based, context-based, and custom metrics usable from CLI/config workflows.

title: Evaluation of RAG pipelines with Ragas - Langfuse
note: Official Langfuse cookbook showing trace/span scoring and Ragas-based RAG evaluation over production traces.

title: comet-ml/opik GitHub repository
note: Official Opik repository describing observability, automated evaluation, datasets, experiments, RAG metrics, PyTest CI integration, cloud, and self-hosting.

title: The RAG Triad - TruLens
note: Official TruLens concept page defining context relevance, groundedness, and answer relevance for RAG evaluation.

title: Evaluating LLMs and Agents with MLflow
note: Official MLflow GenAI evaluation documentation covering datasets, human feedback, LLM-as-judge scorers, systematic evaluation, and production monitoring.

title: RAG Evaluation - Giskard Documentation
note: Official Giskard open-source RAG evaluation example covering retrieval quality, groundedness, answer quality, out-of-scope questions, and multi-turn RAG tests.

title: Evidently documentation: RAG evals
note: Official Evidently tutorial for evaluating RAG retrieval and generation quality with a local open-source workflow, pandas dataframe output, visual reports, and optional platform upload.

title: W&B Weave documentation: Evaluate RAG applications
note: Official Weave guide showing retrieval-step tracing and RAG response evaluation with LLM judges, context precision, datasets, and scorers.

title: LlamaIndex documentation: Evaluating
note: Official LlamaIndex framework docs covering response evaluation, retrieval evaluation, faithfulness, relevancy, semantic similarity, guideline adherence, synthetic question generation, and retrieval metrics.

title: Haystack documentation: Model-Based Evaluation
note: Official Haystack docs for model-based RAG pipeline evaluation, context relevance, faithfulness/groundedness, local evaluator models, and integrations with Ragas and DeepEval.

title: OpenAI API documentation: Working with evals
note: Official OpenAI Evals API docs describing eval creation/runs and stating the Evals platform deprecation timeline: read-only on 2026-10-31 and shutdown on 2026-11-30.

title: LangSmith for Enterprise
note: Official LangSmith enterprise deployment and user-management evidence: cloud, hybrid, self-hosted, roles, SCIM, SSO, and JIT provisioning.

title: LangSmith Administration Overview
note: Official LangSmith data-retention evidence: Base/Extended trace retention tiers and enterprise customization.

title: Langfuse Access Control (RBAC)
note: Official Langfuse RBAC roles and organization/project access evidence.

title: Langfuse Data Retention
note: Official Langfuse retention evidence, including self-hosted object-delete requirements.

title: Phoenix Authentication
note: Official Phoenix self-hosted user-management and role evidence.

title: Phoenix Privacy
note: Official Phoenix self-hosted privacy and data-control evidence.

title: Set up a self-managed W&B Weave instance
note: Official W&B Weave self-managed deployment requirements and enterprise license dependency.

title: Evidently Open-source vs. Cloud
note: Official Evidently OSS, platform, commercial/enterprise, and RBAC feature evidence; also contains a SaaS availability ambiguity to carry forward.

title: DeepEval Enterprise
note: Official Confident AI platform evidence for cloud/self-hosted deployment, SSO/RBAC/audit logs, SOC 2, GDPR, and custom retention.

title: MLflow Self-Hosting Overview
note: Official MLflow open-source and self-hosting evidence.

title: MLflow Authentication with Username and Password
note: Official MLflow authentication and permissions evidence for self-hosted deployments.

title: Promptfoo Security Policy
note: Official Promptfoo security model evidence: OSS runner is local and not a sandbox for untrusted eval content.

title: Giskard AI Red Teaming and LLM Security Platform
note: Official Giskard Hub enterprise controls evidence: data residency, RBAC, audit trails, identity-provider integration, compliance, and on-premise availability.

title: Evidently Cloud v2 - Documentation
note: Official FAQ says Evidently Cloud v2 launched in April 2025 and that new users are automatically enrolled, which conflicts with other current docs saying Evidently Cloud is no longer available as SaaS.

title: Self-hosting - Evidently Documentation
note: Official setup page repeats that Evidently Cloud is no longer available as SaaS and describes self-hosted platform/UI options.
